title: Graph Algorithms: Traversal Framework Foundation
impact: Foundation for all graph algorithm features; Enables path finding, centrality, and component analysis

tasks.description: Define Graph Traversal Types - Create TraversalOptions interface with maxDepth, includeHierarchy, includeRelations, relationTypes, direction, and stopCondition. Create PathResult, ComponentResult, and CentralityResult interfaces
acceptanceCriteria: TraversalOptions interface with all traversal configuration options
PathResult interface with path, relations, and length
ComponentResult interface with entities, relations, and size
CentralityResult interface with entity and score

tasks.description: Create GraphTraversal Class - Implement new src/memory/core/GraphTraversal.ts with constructor accepting graph and relationIndex, implement getNeighbors() helper that respects TraversalOptions
acceptanceCriteria: GraphTraversal class created with constructor(graph, relationIndex)
Returns unique neighbor names as array

tasks.description: Implement BFS Algorithm - Add bfs() method to GraphTraversal that returns Map<string, number> of visited entities with depths, respect maxDepth and stopCondition options
acceptanceCriteria: Returns Map<string, number> with entity names and depths
Traversal order is correct (level by level)

tasks.description: Implement DFS Algorithm - Add dfs() method to GraphTraversal that returns string[] of visited entities in DFS order, respect maxDepth and stopCondition options
acceptanceCriteria: Returns string[] in DFS traversal order
Uses recursive helper or explicit stack

tasks.description: Add getNeighborsWithRelations Helper - Implement method that returns neighbors with their connecting relations for path reconstruction
testCategories: Includes hierarchy edges as null relations
acceptanceCriteria: Returns Array<{ neighbor: string; relation?: Relation }>
Relation edges include full Relation object

tasks.description: Create GraphTraversal Unit Tests - Comprehensive tests for BFS, DFS, getNeighbors, and traversal options
acceptanceCriteria: BFS tests verify correct depths and order
DFS tests verify correct traversal order
Edge case tests for empty, disconnected, cyclic graphs

successCriteria: GraphTraversal class implemented with BFS and DFS
Traversal respects hierarchy and relation edges
notes: This sprint establishes the foundation for all graph algorithm features. The GraphTraversal class provides BFS and DFS as building blocks for more complex algorithms like shortest path and connected components. The getNeighbors abstraction handles both hierarchy (parent-child) and relation (edge) traversal uniformly. This sprint is independent of Sprints 1-5 (performance optimizations) and can be developed in parallel.
